sections.label: Section 5.6.861b
sections.original: Omitto proloqui ; nam nihil ad hanc rem est , Antipho .
sections.translation: I ’ ll omit telling you that , as it is nothing to the present purpose , Antipho . Just as I was going to the woman ’ s apartments , the boy Mida came running up to me , and caught me behind by my cloak , and pulled me back ; I turned about , and inquired for what reason he stopped me ; he said that it was forbidden for any one to go in to his mistress .

sections.label: Section 5.6.865
sections.original: Sophrona modo fratrem huc , inquit , senis introduxit Chremem ,
sections.translation: Sophrona has just now , said he , introduced here Chremes , the old gentleman ’ s brother , and he said that he was then in the room with them : when I heard this , on tip-toe I stole softly along ; I came there , stood , held my breath , I applied my ear , and so began to listen , catching the conversation every word in this fashion ( shows them ) .

sections.label: Section 5.6.869b
sections.translation: Well done , Geta .

sections.label: Section 5.6.869c
sections.translation: Here I overheard a very pretty piece of business ; so much so that I had nearly cried out for joy .

sections.label: Section 5.6.871
sections.translation: What was it ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.871b
sections.translation: What do you think ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.871c
sections.translation: I don ’ t know .

sections.label: Section 5.6.871d
sections.translation: Why , something most marvelous . Your uncle has been discovered to be the father of your wife , Phanium .

sections.label: Section 5.6.872b
sections.translation: Ha ! what ’ s that you say

sections.label: Section 5.6.873b
sections.original: Cum eius consuevit olim matre in Lemno clanculum .
sections.translation: He formerly cohabited secretly with her mother at Lemnos .

sections.label: Section 5.6.874
sections.original: Somnium . Utine haec ignoraret suum patrem ?
sections.translation: A dream : how could she be ignorant about her own father ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.874b
sections.translation: Be sure ,

sections.label: Section 5.6.875
sections.original: Phormio , esse causae : sed me censen potuisse omnia
sections.translation: Phormio , that there is some reason : but do you suppose that , outside of the door , I was able to understand every thing that passed between them within ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.877
sections.original: Atque hercle ego quoque illam audivi fabulam .
sections.translation: On my faith , I too have heard the same story .

sections.label: Section 5.6.877b
sections.translation: Aye , and I ’ ll give you still further reason for believing it : your uncle in the mean time came out from there ; not long after he returned again , with your father ;

sections.label: Section 5.6.880
sections.original: Ait uterque tibi potestatem eius habendae se dare .
sections.translation: each said that he gave you permission to retain her ; in fine , I ’ ve been sent to find you , and bring you to them .

sections.label: Section 5.6.882
sections.original: Quin ergo rape me : quid cessas ?
sections.translation: Why then carry me off [*] at once ;— why do you delay ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.882b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 5.6.882c
sections.translation: O my dear Phormio , farewell !

sections.label: Section 5.6.883b
sections.original: Vale , Antipho : bene , ita me Di ament , factum : gaudeo
sections.translation: Farewell , Antipho . ( ANTIPHO and GETA go into DEMIPHO ’ S house .) So may the Gods bless me , this has turned out luckily . I ’ m glad of it , that such good fortune has thus suddenly befallen them .

sections.label: Section 5.6.885
sections.original: Summa eludendi occasio est mihi nunc senes ,
sections.translation: I have now an excellent opportunity for diddling the old men , and ridding Phaedria of all anxiety about the money , so that he mayn ’ t be under the necessity of applying to any of his companions . For this same money , as it has been given him , shall be given for good , whether they like it or not : how to force them to this , I ’ ve found out the very way .

sections.label: Section 5.6.890
sections.original: Nunc gestus mihi vultusque est capiendus novus .
sections.translation: I must now assume a new air and countenance . But I ’ ll betake myself off to this next alley ; from that spot I ’ ll present myself to them , when they come out of doors . I sha ’ n ’ t go to the fair , where I pretended I was going .

sections.label: Section 5.7.894
sections.original: Dis magnas merito gratias habeo atque ago ,
sections.translation: I do give and return hearty thanks to the Gods ,

sections.label: Section 5.7.895
sections.original: Quando evenere haec nobis , frater , prospere .
sections.translation: and with reason , brother , inasmuch as these matters have turned out for us so fortunately . We must now meet with Phormio as soon as possible , before he squanders our thirty minae , so that we may get them from him . ( Enter PHORMIO , coming forward , and speaking aloud , as though not seeing them .)

sections.label: Section 5.7.898b
sections.translation: I ’ ll go see if Demipho ’ s at home ; that as to what [*] —

sections.label: Section 5.7.899b
sections.original: At nos ad te ibamus , Phormio .
sections.translation: Why , Phormio , we were coming to you .

sections.label: Section 5.7.900
sections.translation: Perhaps about the very same affair . ( DEMIPHO nods assent .)

sections.label: Section 5.7.900b
sections.translation: I ’ faith ,

sections.label: Section 5.7.900c
sections.translation: I thought so .

sections.label: Section 5.7.901
sections.original: Quid ad me ibatis ? Ridiculum . Verebamini
sections.translation: What were you coming to my house for ? Ridiculous ; are you afraid that I sha ’ n ’ t do what I have once undertaken ? Hark you , whatever is my poverty , still , of this one thing I have taken due care , not to forfeit my word .

sections.label: Section 5.7.905
sections.original: Estne ea , ita ut dixi , liberalis ?
sections.translation: Is she not genteel-looking , [*] just as I told you ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.905b
sections.translation: Very much so .

sections.label: Section 5.7.906
sections.original: Itaque ad vos venio nuntiatum , Demipho ,
sections.translation: And this is what I ’ m come to tell you , Demipho , that I ’ m quite ready ; whenever you please , give me my wife . For I postponed all my other business , as was fit I should , when I understood that you were so very desirous to have it so .

sections.label: Section 5.7.910
sections.original: At hic dehortatus est me ne illam tibi darem ;
sections.translation: But he has dissuaded me from giving her to you . For what , says he , will be the talk among people if you do this ? Formerly , when she might have been handsomely disposed of , then she wasn ’ t given ; now it ’ s a disgrace for her to be turned out of doors , a repudiated woman ; pretty nearly , in fact , all the reasons which you yourself , some little time since , were urging to me .

sections.label: Section 5.7.915
sections.translation: Upon my faith , you are treating me in a very insulting manner .

sections.label: Section 5.7.915b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.915c
sections.translation: Do you ask me ? Because I shall not be able to marry the other person I mentioned ; for with what face shall I return to her whom I ’ ve slighted ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.918
sections.original: Tum autem Antiphonem video ab sese amittere
sections.translation: Then besides , I see that Antipho is unwilling to part with her . ( Aside , prompting DEMIPHO .) Say so .

sections.label: Section 5.7.919b
sections.translation: Then besides , I see that my son

sections.label: Section 5.7.920
sections.original: Invitum sane mulierem ab se amittere .
sections.translation: is very unwilling to part with the damsel . But have the goodness to step over to the Forum , and order this money to be transferred to my account , [*] Phormio .

sections.label: Section 5.7.923
sections.original: Quodne ego discripsi porro illis quibus debui
sections.translation: What , when I ’ ve paid it over to the persons to whom I was indebted ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.924
sections.translation: What ’ s to be done , then ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.924b
sections.translation: If you will let me have her for a wife ,

sections.label: Section 5.7.925
sections.original: Quam despondisti , ducam : sin est ut velis
sections.translation: as you promised , I ’ ll take her ; but if you prefer that she should stay with you , the portion must stay with me , Demipho . For it isn ’ t fair that I should be misled for you , as it was for your own sakes that I broke off with the other woman , who was to have brought me a portion just as large .

sections.label: Section 5.7.930
sections.original: I hinc in malam rein cum istac magnificentia ,
sections.translation: Away with you to utter perdition , with this swaggering , you vagabond . What , then , do you fancy we don ’ t know you , or your doings ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.932b
sections.translation: You are provoking me .

sections.label: Section 5.7.932c
sections.translation: Would you have married her , if she had been given to you ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.933b
sections.translation: Try the experiment .

sections.label: Section 5.7.933c
sections.translation: That my son might cohabit with her at your house , that was your design .

sections.label: Section 5.7.935
sections.translation: Pray , what is that you say ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.935b
sections.translation: Then do you give me my money ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.936
sections.translation: Nay , but do you give me my wife ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.936b
sections.translation: Come before a magistrate . ( Going to seize hold of him .)

sections.label: Section 5.7.937
sections.original: In ius ? enimvero si porro esse odiosi pergitis —
sections.translation: Why , really , if you persist in being troublesome —

sections.label: Section 5.7.938
sections.translation: What will you do ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.938b
sections.translation: What , I ? You fancy , perhaps , just now , that I am the protector of the portionless ; for the well portioned , [*]

sections.label: Section 5.7.940
sections.translation: I ’ m in the habit of being so as well .

sections.label: Section 5.7.940b
sections.translation: What ’ s that to us ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.940c
sections.translation: Nothing at all . I know a certain lady here ( pointing at CHREMES ’ S house ) whose husband had —

sections.label: Section 5.7.941b
sections.translation: Ha !
